name: design-agent-evaluation
description: Evaluation scenarios for the OrgX Design Agent skill. Each scenario tests a different artifact type with realistic inputs and expected quality gates.

scenarios.name: Accessibility Audit — Checkout Flow
scenarios.description: Audit the checkout flow (cart review through order confirmation) for WCAG AA compliance. Tests the agent's ability to identify real accessibility barriers, map them to specific WCAG criteria, and provide actionable remediation with effort estimates.

inputs.scope: Checkout flow: cart review, shipping address, payment, order confirmation

known_issues: Users report difficulty tabbing through the payment form
evidence.tech_stack: Next.js, Tailwind CSS, Radix UI primitives

quality_checks.description: Must identify at least 5 issues given the known problems
Payment form tab order and disappearing errors should surface as critical or major
Issues should reference at least 4 distinct WCAG success criteria
Every remediation must include either a code snippet, CSS property, or ARIA attribute — not just prose
quality_checks.validation: All issues[].remediation contain technical specifics

quality_checks.description: Every issue must have an effort field
quality_checks.validation: All issues[].effort in ['low', 'medium', 'high']

quality_checks.description: Recommendations are ordered by impact (critical fixes first)
quality_checks.validation: First recommendation addresses a critical-severity issue

quality_checks.description: At least one screen reader listed

scenarios.name: Design Tokens — v2 System Migration
scenarios.description: Author a v2 design token package that migrates from flat tokens to a three-layer architecture (primitive, semantic, component). Tests the agent's understanding of token architecture, naming conventions, and multi-platform considerations.

known_issues: Current tokens are flat — no semantic layer
Dark mode requires manual overrides in 47 places
iOS app uses different token names than web
requirements: Target platforms: web (CSS custom properties), iOS (Swift), Android (XML)
Must be backwards-compatible with existing component usage

quality_checks.description: Color tokens must have primitive and semantic layers
quality_checks.validation: tokens.color has 'primitive' and 'semantic' keys

quality_checks.description: Must include dark mode token values or a dark_mode_mapping section
quality_checks.validation: dark_mode_mapping is present OR tokens include dark variants

quality_checks.description: Semantic tokens use purpose-based names (text-primary, surface-default) not color-based names (blue-500)
quality_checks.validation: All semantic token names describe function, not appearance

quality_checks.description: All spacing values are multiples of 4px
quality_checks.validation: Every spacing value mod 4 equals 0

quality_checks.description: Typography tokens include fontFamily, fontSize, lineHeight, and fontWeight
quality_checks.validation: All typography entries have all four properties

quality_checks.description: All color values are valid hex or rgb format
quality_checks.validation: All primitive color values match #RRGGBB or rgb(...) pattern

quality_checks.description: Migration notes or alias mappings from old tokens to new are present
quality_checks.validation: A migration or alias section exists

scenarios.name: Interaction Spec — Multi-step Configuration Modal
scenarios.description: Produce a complete interaction specification for a 3-step modal that collects initiative configuration. Tests the agent's ability to map all states, transitions, keyboard behavior, responsive adaptations, and error handling.

evidence.flow_description: User clicks 'New Initiative' button. Modal opens with 3 steps: 1) Name and description, 2) Assign workstreams, 3) Review and launch. Each step validates before proceeding. User can go back. Escape or backdrop click closes with unsaved changes confirmation.

technical_constraints.form_library: React Hook Form with Zod validation

evidence.user_context: Engineering managers creating initiatives 2-3 times per week

quality_checks.description: Must define at least 5 states: closed, open-step-1, open-step-2, open-step-3, confirming-close
Every state pair reachable in the flow has a defined transition
quality_checks.validation: No orphan states — every state has at least one entry and one exit transition

quality_checks.description: Tab order, Escape, Enter, and back-navigation shortcuts are specified
quality_checks.validation: keyboard_behavior covers Tab, Escape, Enter, and Shift+Tab at minimum

quality_checks.description: Focus trap is true and focus restoration on close is specified
quality_checks.validation: focus_trap is true and escape_handling mentions focus restoration

quality_checks.description: Behavior defined for at least mobile, tablet, and desktop
At least 2 error states with clear recovery paths
Dismissal with unsaved changes triggers confirmation
quality_checks.validation: A confirming-close or similar state handles the unsaved changes case

quality_checks.description: Transitions include duration and easing values
quality_checks.validation: All transitions[] have duration and easing fields

scenarios.name: Dark Mode Audit — Live Dashboard
scenarios.description: Audit the OrgX Live Dashboard for dark mode readiness, including palette mapping, contrast verification, elevation strategy, and image handling. Tests the agent's understanding of dark mode design constraints and common pitfalls.

evidence.current_state: Light mode only. No dark mode support. Users have requested dark mode for extended monitoring sessions.
components_to_audit: Agent activity feed with streaming indicators
evidence.tech_stack: React, Tailwind CSS with CSS custom properties, next-themes

quality_checks.description: Every color in the current palette has a dark mode mapping
quality_checks.validation: All 11 current palette colors have corresponding dark values

quality_checks.description: Dark background must not be #000000
quality_checks.validation: No dark mode surface value is #000000

quality_checks.description: At least 3 elevation levels defined with progressively lighter surfaces
All status colors (success, warning, error, info) verified against dark surfaces
quality_checks.validation: contrast_verification includes entries for all 4 status colors

quality_checks.description: Streaming indicators and activity feed have dark mode specifications (agent-first UI signal)
quality_checks.validation: Issues or adaptations mention streaming/activity patterns

quality_checks.description: Contrast ratios are actual numbers, not estimates
quality_checks.validation: All contrast_verification entries have numeric ratio values

quality_checks.description: Testing checklist has at least 5 specific, executable steps
Image handling covers photos, illustrations, icons, and logos
quality_checks.validation: image_handling has entries for at least 3 image categories

global_checks.description: Every output artifact must pass validate_design.py with zero errors
No TODO, TBD, FIXME, or [fill in] text in any output
At least one learning submitted via mcp__orgx__submit_learning per artifact
Summary includes exactly 3 actionable next steps
Every recommendation references specific evidence or design principle, not opinion
